preFix.title: Willow market basket with lid
preFix.subtitle: A round willow shopping basket with a fitted flat lid and two handles
preFix.excerpt: A round willow market basket 300 mm in diameter and 200 mm deep, with a pair of carved wooden handles and a flat woven lid that clips over the rim. The basket uses bye-stakes for density in the sides and a foot border at the base. An intermediate project for someone who has completed a first small basket.
preFix.sourceNotes: Cooke The Basketmaker (1882) for the round base and bye-stake side method; Beeton's Book of Household Management (1861) for the lidded market basket form.

content.text: A market basket needs more density in the sides than a simple egg basket: the load is heavier and the sides must resist distortion.
to double the number of uprights in the side walls. The flat lid woven separately clips over the finished rim on a short skirt of stakes. This basket uses 2.5 to 3 kg of soaked brown buff

Use 4 to 5 mm rods for base stakes and side stakes; 2.5 to 3 mm weavers for the base weaving and side waling; and 3 mm bye-stakes (same thickness as the side stakes). Soak all rods for one hour and mellow for thirty minutes in a damp cloth before use. If weavers begin to feel stiff or crack mid-row, dampen the dry section with a sponge and continue.

Begin with a round slath of eight base rods in two groups of four. Pierce the first four with the bodkin and thread the second four through at right angles. Tie with pairing weave for two rounds, then open to individual spokes. Continue pairing until the base measures 300 mm across. End with four rows of waling to consolidate the edge.
alongside each base spoke using the bodkin. Kink each stake at the base edge and tie the tops together. Complete the upsett: four rows of three-rod
in each direction. The upsett locks the base edge and pulls the stakes into a vertical plane.

alongside every second main stake. Cut the bye-stakes 30 mm shorter than the main stakes so they finish below the border and do not need to be incorporated into the border weave. Point each bye-stake before insertion and use the bodkin to open a path through the lower waling rows.
Weave 170 mm of side in rand weave, working over the bye-stakes as if they were main stakes. Finish with four rows of three-rod waling at the top edge before bordering.

The handles are carved from dry oak or ash with the sloyd knife: a bar grip 120 mm long and 18 mm round in section, tapering to 10 mm pegs at each end. The pegs are pushed through the border from outside. The flat lid is woven separately on a round slath 320 mm across (20 mm wider than the basket to allow a skirt). A short ring of stakes around the lid perimeter bends down to form a 20 mm skirt that clips over the basket rim.
Grip: four-finger grip on the work; thumb-push grip on the knife. Never make a cut whose blade-path can reach a body part.
Cut direction: push cuts (blade away from body) and thumb-pivot cuts are the default. Pull cuts only with the work braced against the chest and the blade-arc unable to reach anything vital.
Sharpening: a dull tool is more dangerous than a sharp one. Strop every fifteen minutes of carving, hone weekly, oilstone monthly.
First aid: keep a styptic pencil, gauze, and a clean dressing within reach. Apply firm pressure, elevate, and dress. A cut that shows fat or will not close with pressure goes to A&E, not a plaster.
Workspace: stable seat, good light, no children or pets within arm-and-blade reach.

attrs.fix: Insert a round former (a pot or tin of the target diameter) inside the basket during weaving to hold the stakes out. Ease off the weaver tension: willow rands should sit snug, not compressed.
attrs.cause: Weavers pulled too tight or stakes not vertical after the upsett
attrs.symptom: The sides lean inward as weaving progresses

attrs.fix: Wet the lid skirt stakes and kink them more sharply using the bodkin. A 5 mm narrower skirt diameter is enough to give a good clip-over fit.
attrs.cause: Lid base woven slightly larger than the basket, or skirt stakes not kinked tightly enough
attrs.symptom: The lid skirt is too loose on the basket rim

initialFindings.message: PATTERN tutorial has no Method/Steps section and no orderedList — sequential instructions missing
